title: Rochelia cynoglossoides
taxonomy: Rochelia cynoglossoides is classified within the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the borage family. This plant is further classified as belonging to the genus Rochelia. Within the Boraginaceae family, the plant shares characteristics with other species such as Lithospermum and Cynoglossum. The classification is based on the plant's morphological and anatomical features. Its classification as a member of the Boraginaceae family suggests its evolutionary relationships with other plants in this family.

intro: Rochelia cynoglossoides, also known as the Chinese rochelia or tongue-word orchid, is a plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throat, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Rochelia cynoglossoides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Rochelia cynoglossoides is often prepared as a decoction or infused in tea, as well as being used in various herbal formulas to promote overall health and well-being.
